findings_applied.fix: Replaced 'All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput.' with 'Ingestion and transformation stages operate sequentially; delivery begins only after transformation is complete.' — eliminates the direct logical contradiction with Stage 2's sequencing requirement.

Removed the dangling 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' sentence from Stage 4 — Monitoring. Added a new 'Security Considerations' section covering authentication, encryption in transit (TLS), encryption at rest (AES-256), and access control (RBAC).

Corrected the inverted benchmark claim. Changed 'batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB. For larger payloads, streaming is preferred.' to 'streaming is 3× faster than batch for files under 10MB. For larger payloads, batch processing is preferred.' Also added 'Internal' qualifier to 'Benchmarks'.
Removed the unsupported '99.9%' figure. Changed 'reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% compared to unbuffered writes' to 'significantly reduces end-to-end write latency compared to unbuffered writes'.
Replaced undefined 'standard retry policy' with explicit definition: 'an exponential backoff retry policy (up to 3 attempts, doubling delay on each transient failure)'.
Replaced undefined 'Large files' with concrete threshold: 'Files exceeding 100MB are handled separately from the primary transformation path via an overflow queue.'

Standardized on 'pipeline' throughout. Changed Operational Notes bullet 2 from 'The pipeline and workflow are monitored' to 'The pipeline is monitored'. Changed Conclusions from 'real-time streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline' to 'real-time streaming pipeline alongside the existing batch pipeline'.
Expanded ETL acronym on first use: 'ETL' → 'ETL (Extract, Transform, Load)'.
Specified authentication mechanism: 'Authentication is used' → 'API key authentication is used'. This aligns with the Security Considerations section added for F-002.

notes: All 9 findings applied. F-002 required adding a new Security Considerations section — content was derived from standard security patterns (TLS, AES-256, RBAC) as no source material was available; this should be reviewed by the document owner for accuracy. F-009 specifies 'API key authentication' as a concrete but assumed mechanism — the document owner should confirm or update to the actual authentication type. Score estimate reflects: critical contradiction resolved (+15), three high-severity correctness/integrity issues fixed (+18), three medium completeness/consistency issues resolved (+9), two low-severity clarity issues resolved (+4), minus minor uncertainty on assumed security details (-5).
